Matías Caño, otherwise known as Pamplona, is a supporting character who appears in the Bank of Spain robbery in Parts 3, 4 and 5. He helps the gang out with melting and storing the gold and also guards the hostages and has been an associate of Bogotá for six years.

Contents

1.1 Part 3
1.2 Part 4
1.3 Part 5


2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships

4.1 Bogotá


5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
Part 3[]
In his first appearance in the third episode, he is 'picked' as one of the 'volunteers' to work in the gold production area. He refuses to do it by saying he is 'no volunteer' which angers Nairobi and results in Bogotá dragging him downstairs to the other hostages' horror. It is later revealed that was all an act and that he is in on the heist and one of the undercover members hired to help the main gang out.
He spends the majority of Part 3 working in the gold shaft along with Nairobi, Bogotá and the other volunteers. He also assists the gang in helping to recover the Governor.

Part 4[]
In Part 4, he is now guarding the hostages more and that is his main role while the main gang can focus on producing the gold and keeping guard on any threats.
He stays downstairs with the hostages and stands in front of them, holding his gun at all times. When Arturo Román caused a commotion amongst the hostages, Matías approached him to warn him but was disarmed by Arturo. However,

Najwa Nimri Urrutikoetxea (born February 14, 1972) is a Spanish actress and singer who portrays Alicia Sierra on the series Money Heist. She is of Navarrese and Jordanian descent. Nimri's mother is Navarrese and her father is Jordanian. When she was a child, she moved to Bilbao and now lives in Madrid. She was married to Daniel Calparsoro from 1995 to 2000 and had a son named Teo in 2004 (father unknown).
Her first movie was Salto al vacío, the first film by her ex-husband, Daniel Calparsoro. She formed the group Najwajean with Carlos Jean.
She was also in the movie Abre los ojos as Nuria, alongside Penelope Cruz and Eduardo Noriega. She also was in Sex and Lucía as Elena, and was nominated for a Goya Award in the category of Best Supporting Actress.
In 2015, she made her television debut playing Zulema Zahir, the main antagonist of the prison drama series Vis a vis, produced by Globomedia for Antena 3 in its first two seasons, and for FOX Spain, in its last two. In 2017, she took the stage with the musical Drac Pack with Alba Flores.
In 2018, she starred in the movie Quién te cantará as Lila, an amnesiac singer. She was nominated for a Goya Award in the category of best female lead performance. That same year she was in the movie El árbol de la sangre, along with Úrsula Corberó.
In 2019, she joined the cast of Money Heist, where she plays Inspector Alicia Sie

title: Monastery of San Giovanni
text:
Monastery of San Giovanni. Monastery of San Giovanni






Inhabitant(s)
Berlin, The Professor


Location
Italy


Filming location
The Hermitage of San Frutos (Saint Fructus)Abbey de Parraces, Segovia (Interior and Courtyard)





First appearance




We're Back






The Monastery of San Giovanni is the Italian monastery where Berlin lived in the years leading up the heist of the Royal Mint of Spain. He planned the heist of the Bank of Spain there, and it was also where he was married to Tatiana in Berlin's Wedding.
He shared the monastery with actual monks. Among these monks is João.

Trivia[]
The name of the monastery was revealed in the episode It's Dolphin Time when Tokyo said "The first time I heard one could make fire underwater was in the Monastery of San Giovanni. I thought it was as improbable as the fact that I was there, listening to Gregorian chants."
Behind the scenes, the exterior shot takes place in the hermitage of San Frutos (Saint Fructus), which is 87 miles from the city center of Madrid in the province of Segovia. The building was built in the 12th century and is located on the top of a canyon, offering views over the Hoces del Duratón Natural Park.[1][2]
References[]


↑ The Film Locations of La Casa de Papel

↑ La Casa de Papel: real life locations in Segovia

María Pedraza (born January 26, 1996) is a Spanish actress and ballerina. She portrayed Alison Parker on Netflix's Money Heist.

Biography[]
Pedraza is best known for portraying Alison Parker in the Spanish series Money Heist and Marina in the series Élite. She also starred as Triana in the series Toy Boy.
Pedraza was discovered through her Instagram account by film director Esteban Crespo who invited her to attend an audition for the lead role of his debut opera AMAR.
From 2018-2020, Pedraza dated Jaime Lorente.[1][2]

Biography[]
After working in a number of public high schools and being unemployed for almost a year, she finally found her dream job: teaching at Brighton College in Madrid, a well-known institution in Spain. She teaches philosophy, ethics and the history of religions. She also runs workshops on the environment and sex education.

Physical Appearance[]
Mercedes has brown hair.

Personality[]
Mercedes cares about her students. She is somewhat timid, but is brave when it comes to defending her students. She insisted on seeing Silvia after she was taken away.

Biography[]
Rio was born in Getafe. His nickname given by his mother was Rayo, meaning lightning bolt in Spanish. At young age he became interested in computers and began programming at the age of 6.[5] He often locked himself in his room for hours. His parents believed that he was working or playing on his computer, but he was actually beginning his career as a hacker.[6]

Biography[]
Amanda is the secretary to the Governor of the Bank of Spain, Mario Urbaneja. She appears in flashbacks of Berlin, when he was attending a meeting with the Governor of the Bank of Spain. She is seen throughout the heist with Arturo Román. He had given her anxiety sleep inducing pills, using them later to take advantage of her while she was knocked out.

Physical Appearance[]
Amanda's main appearances are in a red jumpsuit, as a hostage of many under the heist. 

Personality[]
Amanda is a nervous person.

Suárez is a character in the Netflix series, Money Heist. 
He is a member of the Spanish Police Force. He is part of the police intervention group, Grupo Especial de Operaciones.

Contents

1 Biography

1.1 Part 1


2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships
5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
Part 1[]
Suárez was in charge of the operations of the Grupo Especial de Operaciones during the Royal Mint of Spain robbery.
Under the instructions of Raquel Murillo, he was ordered to shoot a masked person when several hostages and robbers went onto the roof.[3] However, this person turned out to be Arturo Román, a hostage. He was later treated by a medical team.[4]
He later tried to enter the mint with Agent García Lobo to infiltrate the robbery from the inside, by wearing Dalí masks. However, the Professor, being aware of the police plan, warns the group of robbers.  All the hostages and robbers change their masks to a mask resembling The Scream by Edvard Munch. The police immediately abort the operation.[4]
Later, he takes over control of operations with Alfonso Prieto when Raquel Murillo is suspected of complicity with the robbers.
When the robbers leave the Factory, he leads a group of police officers to enter the scene. They will eventually bring down Berlin who dies instantly, who stayed to allo

text:
Browning Machine Gun. Browning Machine Gun

Behind the scenes

The "Browning" Machine Gun was a heavy machine gun utilised in the robbery of the National Bank of Spain.

Contents

1 History

1.1 Before Money Heist
1.2 Part 1
1.3 Part 2
1.4 Part 3


2 Trivia


History[]
Before Money Heist[]
The Machine gun was bought on the dark web by Rio, guided by the professor. 

Part 1[]
The Browning is first seen at the first attempt of the police to break into the bank. As they use a micro-camera to look through the door, they see hostages and robbers, all dressed up the same, aiming their guns at them and the middle one revealing the Browning. Out of fear for the machine gun the police decides to retreat.

Part 2[]
The Browning appears again when the police tries to get in to the bank through a hole made by escaping hostages. As the robbers are under heavy fire, Tokyo runs back and returns with the machine gun. When she starts shooting, the police retreats again.
It’s last appearance happens in the last episode of Part 2. As the robbers are preparing to leave the bank, Berlin orders Helsinki to get the Browning. 
Later, when almost everybody has gone through the tunnel, Berlin reveals he plans to stay behind with Ariadna to hold off the police. As the others leave after some talking, Berlin takes his final stand behind the machine gun.
As the police rushes in, Berlin starts shooting, making the police hide behind the corners. After numerous failed attempts to rush to him while he is reloading, they decide to blow up the gun with grenades. Which they do, sealing it’s fate.

Part 3[]
A similar gun, likely the same model, appears in Part 3, also referred to as the “Browning”.

Trivia[]
Even though it is identified as a "Browning" by Suarez, it is in fact a german MG-42 machine gun from the World War 2 era.

Enrique Arce (born October 8, 1972) is a Spanish television and film actor. He portrays Arturo Román in the series Money Heist.
Arce was born in Valencia, Spain on October 8, 1972. He originally went to university to study law, but during his fourth year, he decided to move into acting. He won "Ellos i Elles", a Valencian television show on Canal Nou, and used the 2 million pesetas (circa £10,000/$15,000) prize money to pay for his studies at the American Academy of Dramatic Arts. He has developed his career in Spain and abroad.
He was married to the actress Cristina Peña, and lived with Olympic synchronised swimmer Gemma Mengual from 2005 to 2008.
On April 25, 2020, he gave a shoutout to the Filipino fans and is shocked to be messaged by Filipino celebrity Lovi Poe.

title: Royal Mint of Spain
text:
Royal Mint of Spain. Royal Mint of Spain






Type
Mint


Location
Madrid, Spain


Filming location
Spanish National Research Council





First appearance
Latest appearance




Episode 1 (Part 1)
Episode 9 (Part 2)





The Royal Mint of Spain, also known as the Fábrica Nacional de Moneda y Timbre (National Factory of Coins and Stamps), is a government-owned institution responsible for producing and distributing currency and stamps in Spain. The mint has a long history, dating back to the 16th century when King Felipe II established the Casa de la Moneda (House of Coins) in Madrid.
Today, the Royal Mint of Spain is a modern facility that uses state-of-the-art technology to produce coins and other currency items. It is also responsible for creating and producing official seals, documents, and stamps, including the highly-regarded Spanish postage stamps. The Royal Mint can produce up to €2.4 billion in the span of 128 hours, depending on various factors such as the type and denomination of the money being produced.
The building itself is located in Madrid and is an imposing neoclassical structure with a grand entrance and an expansive interior. Over the years, the building has been renovated and expanded to accommodate modern production processes and security measures, though none of it was enough to stop the robbers from infiltrating and taking multiple hostages.
The actual Royal Mint of Spain's building is not in Money Heist, but instead the Spanish National Research Council.

History[]
In 2016, it was the location of a heist, where a group of robbers who took 67 hostages while staying inside the bank for 11 days in the Royal Mint of Spain Heist. They printed €984 million while in the mint.
During the heist, the robbers force some hostages to dig a tunnel as a red herring, while Moscow digs into an already built tunnel from one of the vaults, connecting to the professor's hangar. This tunnel is the robber's intended escape route.

Biography[]
On October 21, 2016, when Pablo was 17, he visited the Royal Mint of Spain on a school excursion. During the heist, he is taken hostage like the rest of his classmates. He is forced to give the robbers his phone and wear a red jumpsuit.
After Pablo reveals to the robbers that he is captain of the Brighton College athletic team, he is chosen, along with Arturo Román, to dig a tunnel.
Along with Aurora, Elisabeth and a couple other classmates, he tries to intimidate Alison Parker after learning that the police have decided to save her alone, instead of eight hostages.[2]
During the heist, Arturo Román convinces Pablo to take part in an escape plan. Pablo co-operates and leaves tools in the toilet, so that they can be retrieved by Jacinto, who then passed the tools to the other hostages that are tied up. After Jacinto explodes one of the doors of the mint, Pablo, along with 15 other hostages, manages to escape, much to the surprise of the police.
After escaping from the Royal Mint of Spain, along with some other hostages, he tells the police about the tunnel that he dug with Arturo.

Physical Appearance[]
Pablo is blonde. 

Personality[]
Pablo is confident and enjoys being at the centre of attention.

Relationships[]
Alison Parker[]

Part 4





Part 5 (also referred to as season 3) of Netflix's Money Heist. Volume 1 was released on September 3, 2021 and Volume 2 was released on December 3, 2021. It is the final season of Money Heist.

Contents

1 Synopsis
2 Plot
3 Cast

3.2 Recurring Cast

Synopsis[]
The gang has been shut in the Bank of Spain for over 100 hours. They have managed to rescue Lisbon, but their darkest moment is upon them after losing one of their own. The Professor has been captured by Sierra and, for the first time, doesn’t have an escape plan. Just when it seems like nothing else could go wrong, an enemy comes on the scene that is much more powerful than any they’ve faced: the army. The end of the greatest heist in history is approaching, and what began as a robbery will turn into a war.[1]

She was born as Ágata Jiménez. Due to her poor conditions, she learned to counterfeit money at 13. During her teenage years, she had a boyfriend, who she later became pregnant with. Her boyfriend left after he found out about the pregnancy. Jiménez gave birth to a boy, she named him Axel. However, when Axel was 3, child services took him away from her after finding out she was dealing drugs.[5]

Biography[]
When Sergio was a child, he suffered a serious illness. To raise enough money to fund his son's treatment at an American hospital, Jesús robbed banks. At his bedside, he told Sergio about his robberies and his dreams of robbing a mint one day.[2]
On February 5, 1987, Jesús Marquina left home to rob the Spanish-American bank. Sergio, not knowing what his father was up to, followed him by bicycle. He watched as his father ran out of the bank holding bags of cash in his hands. Police point their guns at him and order that he surrender, but Jesús notices Sergio watching him and gets distracted. Jesús fails to follow police orders and is shot dead.

Physical Appearance[]
Jesús has dark hair and wears glasses.

Personality[]
Relationships[]
Not much is known about Jesús Marquina's relationships except that he was a loving father who cared deeply about his sons,  and was willing to rob banks and risk his life to secure a future for them as well as providing enough money to pay for Sergio’s treatment.

Biography[]
In 1989, Francisco was hired at the Royal Mint of Spain.
Francisco has 3 daughters. One of them has been unemployed for 7 years.[1]

Part 1[]
On the morning of October 21, 2016, Francisco Torres went to work at the mint as usual, but wound up becoming a hostage in a heist. At the time of the heist, Francisco had worked at the mint for 27 years.[2] He was the oldest out of everyone. 
During the heist, Nairobi put him in charge of printing counterfeit banknotes. Later, on October 23, he was named "hostage of the year" for printing €311 million in less than 40 hours, breaking his own recording in printing.
Later, when the hostages were offered a choice between money and freedom, Francisco did not hesitate to continue printing banknotes at the mint, because printing counterfeit banknotes pleased him much more than anything else he had done in his life, and he had never had a leader like "Miss Nairobi" before.[3]

Part 2[]
On October 25, 2016, Nairobi summons Francisco to her office, asking if it is possible to increase the printing speed. Torres says yes, but states that if something goes wrong, it will take three hours to get the machines working again. Nairobi then asks him to calculate the possible increase in production.[1]
Later, he discovers an open bag full of weapons in a nearby office, and Arturo Román, his boss, is hidden under the desk. Arturo is preparin

On October 21,  2016, Jacinto was taken hostage during the heist. As Jacinto was considered to be potentially dangerous, he was tied up in a basement with other security guards.
Initially, he poses no threat to the robbers. When inspector Raquel Murillo entered the mint to personally ensure that the hostages were well, he told Raquel that his general condition was good.
However, a few hours later Arturo Román convinces him to take part in an escape plan. Arturo gets Pablo Ruiz to hide tools in the toilet, so that Jacinto can retrieve them later. Jacinto attaches the tools to his body and brings them to the other hostages. While unguarded, the hostages manage to remove their restrains. When Oslo returns and sees the hostages untied, before he can react, Jacinto hits him on the back of his head. The hostages supposed to meet Arturo at a scheduled meeting point. However, Arturo is not there. They wait for him for a few minutes, and end up deciding to escape without him.
Along with 15 other hostages, Jacinto reaches one of the doors of the mint, which is full of plastic explosives. Jacinto manages to explode the door without injuring any hostages, and they escape through the hole under the astonished gaze of the police.
After Jacinto escapes, he explains to the police everything he remembers about the inside of the mint.

Physical Appearance[]
Jacinto has brown hair.

On October 21, 2016, Silvia visited the Royal Mint of Spain on a school excursion. During the heist, she is taken hostage.

On Sunday October 23, 2016, Berlin, one of the robbers, had fun scaring this group of hostages by revealing to them that he had Mónica Gaztambide executed and that he did not regret it. Silvia begins to have a panic attack and Berlin takes her to his office to increase the other girls’ anxiety. She finds herself tied up in the Berlin office and subjected to Berlin's inappropriate gestures.[1]

Biography[]
A few days before the heist, the Professor gave Helsinki some money and tasked him with taking the Professor's red Seat Ibiza to the car scrapyard and watch it be crushed in front of his eyes. However, Helsinki chose to send the money to his family instead, and leaves the car at the scrapyard completely intact.[1]
During the heist, the police find that the Seat Ibiza from 1992 was driven by Tokyo and Rio during their visit to the Royal Mint of Spain before the heist, so they decide to go to the nearby scrap yards to find it. The Professor learns of the police's plan and hurries to the scrapyard to find the car and erase any clues. But he is surprised to see Nikolai, who watches over the place. As the scrapyard is closed to the public, he sneaks inside and hides in a car. Nikolai, however, sees him and scares the Professor by crushing the car that he is hiding in. The Professor tries to offer him some money, but due to not having enough, Nikolai lets his dog chase him. Later, the Professor returns a second time, and manages to clean the vehicle of fingerprints before Nikolai finds it. By the time the police, the car has already been wiped down, and the Professor has disguised himself.[1]
Later, Nikolai goes to the police tent outside the Royal Mint of Spain, and helps the police by trying describing the Professor's appearance so that the police and create a composite ske

Biography[]
Miguel is from Totana, Murcia.[1][2]
Miguel was working as an intern in the Operations and Information Systems department when the heist at the Bank of Spain occurred. At the beginning of the heist, Bogotá and Nairobi considered designating him as a volunteer for manual labour, but later deemed him unfit for the task.
Later, when the robbers order all hostages to put on a bullet proof vest and carry a fake gun, Miguel warns the hostages not to and claims that the robbers will use them as human shields. Upon hearing this, Denver orders Miguel to put two bullet vests and makes his guess the name of a movie. Miguel guesses incorrectly and says "WALL-E" instead of "The Terminator", so Denver pulls the trigger of his gun and shoots Miguel, sending him flying backwards. Miguel is mostly unharmed, albeit shaken from the incident.[3]

Personality[]
Miguel is an awkward and nervous man, although he has short bursts of courage every now and then, especially when he is trying to impress Amanda.

Biography[]
Mari Carmen was married to Ángel. However, she knows that her husband has had feelings for his colleague, Raquel for many years, so she holds a grudge against her.
On Sunday October 23, 2016, she called her husband when he went to the pharmacy in Palomeque.
During the heist at the Royal Mint of Spain, Ángel is distraught when Raquel suspects him of being a mole and confesses his love for her. He claims that he would leave Mari Carmen instantly if Raquel was willing to be with him. However, while driving, Ángel loses control of his car and ends up in a coma.
Shortly after the accident, Mari Carmen visits him in the hospital and meets Raquel there. Mari Carmen criticises Raquel for the fact that Ángel has ben in love with her for so long, implying that she is responsible for the accident. She confides in Raquel that she has waited 7 years for Ángel to get over his feelings for her.[1]
Later, Mari Carmen leaves Ángel.[2]

Physical Appearance[]
Mari Carmen has short reddish-brownish hair.

Personality[]
Relationships[]
Ángel Rubio[]
Mari was married to Ángel during the Bank of Spain heist. After finally having enough of Ángel's unloyal behavior in their relationship, she divorced him. It is assumed Ángel and Mari have not spoken since.

Biography[]
Mario Urbaneja is the governor at the Bank of Spain at the time of the Bank of Spain heist. He employs his secretary Amanda and is protected by five body guards led by César Gandía. Years before the Heist, Urbaneja has a meeting with Berlin, whose scouting for the heist.
Urbaneja was targeted by the robbers because he has a key to the closet of state secrets in the vault. Tokyo and Nairobi were sent to capture him, but were initially thwarted by Gandía. Even though, he was eventually captured by the robbers. He was subsequently brought down to the vault and was told to enter the vault via a pressure tube in a scuba suit. When he says no he is almost fatally injured by Denver. He survives and then is left with the rest of the hostages.
When the hostages are moved to the library, Urbaneja is one of the hostages along with Miguel Fernández Talanilla who confront Arturo Román for raping Amanda and attempting to rape Manila.

Physical Appearance[]

title: The Toledo House
text:
The Toledo House. The Toledo House






Inhabitant(s)
HelsinkiNairobiTokyoRioDenverThe ProfessorMoscowOsloBerlin


Location
Toledo, Spain


Filming location
Finca El Gasco





First appearance
Latest appearance




Episode 1 (Part 1)
TKO (flashback)





The Toledo House was the location where the original robbers stayed for five months in preparation for the Royal Mint of Spain heist. The house is mostly isolated from the surrounding area of Palomeque, a principality in the province of Toledo.[1] The house can have about nine people living there at a time. The most notable room is the classroom. The room’s walls are covered in the plans for the heist. There are eight desks, a chalk board, tables, and a model of the Royal Mint of Spain. The Professor teaches the robbers everything they need to know about the heist in that room.

Contents

1 History

1.1 June 2016
1.2 October 2016


2 Gallery
3 References


History[]
June 2016[]
In June 2016, the Professor recruited eight robbers including Silene Oliveira. He brings these eight individuals into this house, for a period of five months.[2]
Within this 640 ha property[3], the Professor explains to them the details of the heist plan. The robbers each have their own room.[2]

October 2016[]
Raquel realises that the robbers needed a place to plan and practice shooting for their heist. Therefore, she asks all the teams to look for a property with a shooting area around Palomeque pharmacy where Retroxil was purchased. The police scoured the city of Toledo and ended up finding the house where the robbers had trained. 
Arriving on the scene, Raquel, the forensic police, and the teams of Suárez discover that there are empty bottles that could give the DNA of the robbers and even the sales records of the Palomeque pharmacy. The Professor's entire plan seems to have been uncovered. In reality, the Professor has, once again, planned everything. He purposely left the police a crime scene filled with small unnecessary details to gives the robbers t

Biography[]
Sofía is a ferret belonging to Marseille. She assists the robbers once Rio is returned to the group. She is given Rio's tracking and surveillance chip, and sent throughout the tunnels underneath the Bank of Spain with it to trick Alicia Sierra and Luis Tamayo. She is captured once the police are able to locate her.

Physical Appearance[]
Sofía looks like a ferret. She has brown and white fur. She also has her own little red jumpsuit.

Personality[]
Relationships[]
Trivia[]
Denver referred to Sofía as a rat in It's Dolphin Time.
Sofia is the capital of Bulgaria, so Sofía follows the code of using city names. However this likely wasn't on purpose, considering Marseille seemingly owned the ferret prior to the heist, and unlike other city names, Sofía is also a common female name, for example two spanish royals have that name.

text:

Type

Royal Mint of Spain Heist is a heist that took place from Friday October 21, 2016 around 10 a.m. to Wednesday October 26, 2016 at 6:32 p.m.

Contents

1 Context
2 Notable Participants

2.1 Robbers
2.2 Hostages
2.3 Police

8 Requests

14 Cameroon Plan

Context[]
On October 21, 2016 at around 10 a.m., eight robbers entered the Royal Mint of Spain, following the Professor's orders. Their goal is to print €2.4 billion in small denominations of 50 euros, in less than eleven days, and that without shedding a single drop of blood. However, they will have to watch over sixty-seven hostages.
The mastermind of this plan was Jesús Marquina, the Professor's father.

The Professor's Plan[]
By recruiting his eight robbers, the Professor explains from the start that he does not want names, personal questions, or intimate relationships between the robbers: NPPRP (Ningún Nombre, Preguntas Personales, o Relaciónes Personales).
The goal is to hold up the Royal Mint of Spain in Madrid t

Biography[]
Pilar works in forensics, under Alberto Vicuña. She is tasked with investigating the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, so she first inspects the police car used by the Professor to intimidate Nikolai Dimitrievich. She manages to isolate a few fingerprints and traces of DNA, but unfortunately none matches the DNA listed in the police files.
Later, unofficially, Ángel Rubio asks her to compare the DNA found in the car with a sample found on a teaspoon stolen from Salva, aka the Professor. He wants to prove to Raquel that "Salva" is connected with the heist.
Some time later, Pilar calls Ángel back and confirms that the person who touched the spoon is the one who got into the police vehicle. However, he gets into a car accident and falls into a coma before he can tell Raquel about this.

Biography[]
Laura was married to Arturo Román for 14 years. Together, they had three children. During that time, Arturo had an affair with his secretary, Mónica Gaztambide, but Laura was not aware. When Arturo was shot by a police sniper and need medical help he was allowed by the robbers to call his wife. During the call Arturo confess to Laura that he has been a bad husband and that they will go on a vacation to Australia together after the heist was over to visit Laura's sister. At the end of the call Arturo says Mónica's name instead of Laura's.[1]
After the heist, Laura divorces from Arturo and takes major custody of the children.[2]

Relationships[]
Arturo Román[]
At the time of the Bank of Spain Heist, Arturo and Laura had been married for 14 years and had 3 children together.[3] However, Arturo had been cheating on Laura with Mónica. The two later divorced.

Trivia[]
Laura has a sister who lives in Australia.[1]

Money Heist: Korea - Joint Economic Area (Korean: 종이의 집: 공동경제구역) is a Korean adaptation of Money Heist that is created by Kim Hong-sun, Ryu Yong-jae, Kim Hwan-chae, and Choe Sung-jun. It set on the Korean peninsula, and the shows’ creators will breathe new life into the familiar storyline.[2] It was announced in December 2020.[3]
The first season of the Korean adaptation will have 12 episodes. Each episode will have a runtime of 60 minutes.[4]

Synopsis[]
The enigmatic Professor assembles a group of skilled thieves from the North and South to steal the freshly minted currency of a to-be unified Korea.
Money Heist: Korea - Joint Economic Area takes place as North and South Korea are on the verge of a peaceful reunification after nearly 80 years of division. As the nations prepare to print a brand-new unified currency, a heist is hatched. A genius strategist known as the Professor assembles a motley crew of top-tier thieves from north and south in an attempt to steal the new currency straight from the mint.
The Professor (Yoo Ji-tae) who approaches Tokyo (Jun Jong-seo) with a plan to steal 4 trillion won from the Korea Unified Mint. He assembles a top-class group of thieves to pull it off, but chaos ensues as hostages are taken and a joint task force led by South Korean negotiati

Bank of Spain Heist is a heist that began on April 4th, 2019 and lasted approximately seven days.

Contents

Context[]
Rio is captured by Europol while he is in Guna Yala. Tokyo meets the Professor at the Sanctuary of Truth in Thailand and tells him about the news. They predict that Rio is being tortured for information as the police have not released any information about his capture. The Professor reunites the robbers from the Royal Mint of Spain, Lisbon (former police officer Raquel Murillo) and Stockholm (Mónica Gaztambide, Denver's wife) and explains his plan to save Rio. He recalls a plan to rob the Bank of Spain that he and Berlin discussed while they were in Italy.

title: Discord
text:
Discord. Administration
Policies • Layout Guide • Design • Discord • Content Moderator Applications

Community
Blogs • Discussions • Userboxes • Templates

The Money Heist Wiki Discord is a place to chat, share news and discuss wiki-related topics.
This server intended for Money Heist fans and is not affiliated with Money Heist or Netflix. On this server, we intend to follow Money Heist Wiki site policies, as well as server rules.
Concerns or suggestions regarding Discord can be discussed on the talk page.


Join the chat!
How to join[]
To join, click the button above or this link https://discord.com/invite/spYckSV.

How to verify[]
To verify, go to #🆔│verification. In this channel, you should type !wiki verify <wiki_username>.
For example, if you wiki username is Nairobi100, you should type !wiki verify Nairobi100.
Note: Verified users will receive the @Wiki User role. Users with at least 100 edits or posts will receive the @Wiki Pro role.
Note: Users who do not wish to to publicize their Discord name may remove it from their masthead after verification.

Rules[]
1 - Respect Others.
Treat every user with respect. No discrimination, bigotry, harassment, etc. We have a no-tolerance policy for this.

2 - Spoilers
Avoid posting spoilers outside of the "Spoiler Zone" category. We want everyone to have the best experience on their first watch.

3 - Value Privacy.
Don't share any personal information of yourself or others. Examples may include addresses, phone numbers, images and other information.

4 - No advertising.
While we encourage you to post money heist-related media, please avoid advertising your own products, domains, discord servers, etc. If you are not sure if something is allowed contact a staff member.

5 - No NSFW Content.
NSFW Content (Sexual content, gore, illegal content, shock-inducing media) is not welcome here.

6 - Do not excessively spam.
Don't flood chats or post long copypasta's.

7 - Common Sense.
Use common sense. Don't try to find loopholes in the rules

Tokyo's Mother is the mother of the robber, Tokyo.

Contents

1 Biography

3 Personality

Biography[]
Tokyo's mother raised her daughter as a single mother. She worked in a suitcase factory, and when she was struggling financially, she had to work the night shift to keep her and her daughter afloat. Therefore, Tokyo spent her days alone when she was only 8 or 9 years old. To combat her daughter's loneliness, Tokyo's mother painted a door in her room as a way to reassure her, telling her she can open the door once and Tokyo's mother will come right to her.
After her daughter's robbery of a van, she is being pursued by the police. The police contact Tokyo's mother to get to Tokyo and Tokyo's mother agrees to help. Tokyo decides to call him, believing that her mother is alone and staying in a place to see each other. After their conversation, Tokyo's mother does not warn him of the trap and hangs up the phone. 
Before the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, Tokyo's mother died of cardiac arrest. Tokyo denies at first, feeling resentment because she tried to hand her over to the police. Later in the heist, Tokyo confesses to Rio, saying she is actually quite sad about her mother's death.

Biography[]
Paco Cortés is the father of Aníbal Cortés. When he discovers his son's participation in the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, he gives an interview with his wife on television about his son. In the interview he talks about how he always knew his son was in his room on his computer, but he and his wife just believed he was playing video games, not committing crimes. Paco gets quite heated in the interview, even going so far as to confess that for him, "his son is dead." 
However, hours later they change their minds. They hire a lawyer and are trying to get their son to turn himself in and have a reduced sentence. They meet with the police, who manage to get their son a video to try to convince him to turn himself in.

Biography[]
Rio's Mother is the mother of Aníbal Cortés. When Aníbal was young, he told his mother he wanted to be an olympian. Rio's mother often called Aníbal, Rayo as a nickname.
When she discovers her son's participation in the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, she gives an interview with her husband on television about their son. In the interview she talks about her son with her husband and how they just believed he was playing video games on his computer, not committing crimes. The interview gets quite heated in the interview, with Paco Cortés even going so far as to confess that for him, "his son is dead," to which Rio's mother finds her husband has gone too far.
Hours later, they hire a lawyer and are trying to get their son to turn himself in and have a reduced sentence. They meet with the police, who manage to get their son a video to try to convince him to turn himself in.

title: Car Junkyard
text:
Car Junkyard. This article is a stub. You can help the Money Heist Wiki by  expanding it.



Car Junkyard

Type
Car junkyard





First appearance
Latest appearance




Episode 7 (Part 1)
Episode 7 (Part 1)





The Car Junkyard (  Spanish:   desguace ) is the place where the Professor's red Ibiza car was abandoned. The place is guarded by Nikolai Dimitrievich.

Contents

1 History
2 Employees
3 Visitors
4 Gallery


History[]
Prior to Season 1, Helsinki was tasked with taking the Professor's car to the junkyard and have it crushed in front of his eyes. He was paid €1000 to do so, but Helsinki sent the money to his family instead, and simply left the car intact in the junkyard.
When the Professor hears of this, he goes to the junkyard to find the car. However, he is spotted by Nikolai Dimitrievich who chases him away. The Professor later returns more discreetly and finds the red car. He begins to remove traces of DNA and fingerprints with ammonia. He also intentionally leaves a button from Berlin's jacket there to punish him, as he believed that he had ordered the execution of a hostage.

Employees[]
Nikolai Dimitrievich
Visitors[]
The Professor
Raquel Murillo
Unnamed police officers

1 Biography
2 Physical Appearance

Biography[]
Sagasta is a commander of the Special Forces of the Spanish Army. He is hardened in countless international missions against the worst of the human species. Which places him, precisely, in the same place as the men he has killed. He is a born leader, who his men will follow to the end if he asks. He is first seen at his home on leave at his bathroom, contacted by Colonel Tamayo to bring him his team to defeat the Professor's team. When he arrives, he is assigned to lead Special Forces Unit 3 led by Canizo. However, he refuses and demands his own team, but Tamayo refuses to because of the previous crimes his soldiers have committed. Sagasta tells him that he knows every detail about his team and that they are just outside. 
During the operation, Tokyo's suicide bombing wiped out the majority of his team. Sagasta, Arteche and Vazquez were the only survivors of the bombing due to their distance from the bombing.

Physical Appearance[]
Sagasta has short dark hair.

The End of the Road (  Spanish:   El final del camino ) is the first episode of Part 5 of Netflix's Money Heist. It aired on September 3, 2021.

Contents

1 Synopsis
2 Plot
3 Cast

3.1 Main Cast
3.2 Supporting Cast
3.3 Minor Cast


4 Quotes
5 Trivia
6 Gallery
7 References


Synopsis[]
The Professor faces off with Sierra as Tamayo raises the stakes of the negotiations by calling the army. In the past, Berlin meets with a family member.

Plot[]
The opening begins with Alicia Sierra reflects what Tamayo did to her and walks at the abandoned storm tank. During the celebration Alicia spots the Professor and before the Professor reacts, she points him at gunpoint, mocking him and telling the genius that he’s living like a rat. She wants his entire plan, and then she plans to get him convicted for terrorism. Professor attempts to get out of this extremely sticky situation, and the pair fight, but of course, Sierra wasn’t going to give up so easily.
In flashbacks, Berlin meets his son Rafael, it’s clear they haven’t seen each other for a long time, so they are reconciling. Berlin reveals that he has forgiven Rafael for being born, as his birth completely destroyed Berlin's 'exquisite' relationship with his ex-wife (Rafael's mother). Berlin’s wife Tatiana shows up looking classy and sexy, which slightly annoys Rafael. Berlin’s usual sinister side comes out as he asks Rafael if he’d sleep with Tatiana. if he'd not be married to her. The conversation then turns to Berlin trying to convince Rafael to do a heist with him as he’s a cyber security engineer from MIT, Massachusetts. Rafael calls Berlin a despicable and selfish

4 Quotes

Synopsis[]
A betrayal and an emergency both catch Sierra by surprise. Palermo rallies the troops as the army closes in on the gang.

Plot[]
Stockholm revive Arturo after shooting him. At last Arturo got the pulse. Stockholm demands to free Arturo so that he can take to the hospital. Palmero was convinced to release Arturo by Lisbon. The Governor was released with Arturo and some hostages. Tamayo wants everyone to think the heist team are murderers, and he holds a press conference, calling Sierra a traitor. If Sierra's captured, she will be imprisoned. Her water breaks at the Professor's place. The flashback of  Denver, Benjamin and Moscow shows. Professor tries to convince Sierra to join them as she will be in prison if she not joined. Sierra is in labour pain. 
Tamayo plans the attack with his army unit led by Sagasta. Inside the bank, Palmero convince the heist team an attack for an attack. Captain Gandia also joins Sagasta's team. Sagasta's plans to enter into the building from the roof only. At last Tamayo plans to blow up the roof. 
Professor helps Sierra in her childbirth. The heist team get ready to attack the army. They plan not to stop the gold melting on any attack. The army starts the operation by blowing the roof.

Main Cast[]

Álvaro Morte as The Pro

Synopsis[]
With Helsinki's life in danger, the thieves mount a covert counter attack. Years earlier, Berlin and his son conduct their first heist together.

Plot[]
Helsinki get Injured because of roof blowing. The professor again gains control and starts guide the team. But The heist team is surrounded by the military.
In the flashback, Berlin plan to conduct a heist with his team, son Patrick Criado (Rafael) and wife. They did it successfully but Berlin teach a lesson to his son of Patrick's arrogance by throwing a gold suitcase into the water. But later they take the gold suitcase back.  
In the present, the fight continues. Helsinki's condition is getting worst. The heist team reaches near to Helsinki. Helsinki tells Palermo to cut his leg as it is very hard to move the large marble statue from his leg. On the side, professor gives order to set free the all hostages without masks to distract the snipers on the roof. Lisbon did a trick by putting a mic in governor's handcuff.  All the hostages and the heist team (Tokyo, Manila and Denver) come on the roof and remove the masks. Palermo's team tried to remove the status by using a beam.   
Professor calls Tamayo and make him to hear Tamayo's recording recorded by the mic in the handcuff. Professor threats Tamayo to stop the attack. Tamayo gives an order to

Live Many Lives (  Spanish:   Vivir muchas vidas ) is the fifth episode of Part 5 of Netflix's Money Heist. It aired on September 3, 2021.

Contents

1 Synopsis
2 Plot
3 Cast

3.1 Main Cast
3.2 Supporting Cast
3.3 Minor Cast


4 Quotes
5 Trivia
6 Gallery
7 References


Synopsis[]
While surrounded by gunfire and trapped in the kitchen, Tokyo reminisces about her first love and her initial encounter with the Professor.

Plot[]
In the flashback, Tokyo's first love René got killed. Professor and Berlin are watching the Tokyo's heist news in TV. Tokyo is running away from the police after unsuccessful heist.  
In present, the firing continues. Rio tries to hack the door system of museum. Tokyo engages the military team by targeting Gandia. Gandia throws a grenade and that returns back to them. Two from military team got  wounded and one dead. Stockholm cures Helsinki. Arteche and Gandia starts fighting with each other. They stop fighting and take positions. The heist team tries to cut the door. Professor introduces Sierra and his new born daughter to the team. They give her a name Victoria.
The military team did holes to kitchen. Tokyo fires through hole and hits at knee on the other side. Tokyo runs away but The snipers start firing. Tokyo gets shot five bullets. Rio tries to break the ceiling to save Tokyo. Monica gave an idea to use service life to get out from the kitchen. Tokyo asked everybody to leave as she can not get downstairs through rope to four floor. Rio did a hole into kitchen's floor. But they enter into the kitchen. Tokyo starts firing alone at them

title: Rainbow Six Siege
text:
Rainbow Six Siege. Rainbow Six Siege is a first person shooter game. It collaborated with Money Heist to make a limited time event from November 20 - November 25, 2019. It was introduced during the Operation Ember Rise expansion and takes place in the Rainbow Six Siege map: Bank.

Contents

1 Official Information

1.1 Description
1.2 Rules
1.3 Bundles

1.3.1 Hibana Heist Bundle
1.3.2 Vigil Accomplice Bundle




2 Trivia
3 Media

3.1 Images
3.2 Video


4 External Links
5 References


Official Information[]
Description[]
There’s a situation going on at the Bank. A hostage was taken, and it’s up to you to ensure their safety. The heist is ongoing, and the tension is coming to a head.
For Rainbow Six: Siege’s first collaboration with Netflix, we are bringing you a short event themed after their Spanish-language series, Money Heist.[1]

Rules[]
The Money Heist event is a classic Hostage match, where Attackers and Defenders must fight over a Hostage dressed in the series' iconic mask and red jumpsuit. Hibana and Vigil are also equipped with the same look, and players can pick them to play with.[1]

Bundles[]
A full set of four items for both Hibana and Vigil were available for purchase in the shop during the event and were there for approximately a year.[1]

Hibana Heist Bundle[]
Hibana’s Heist Bundle contains the Red Jumpsuit uniform, Emboldened headgear, Tokyo charm and Regal Mint weapon skin for the TYPE-89.[1]

Vigil Accomplice Bundle[]
Vigil’s Accomplice Bundle includes the Red Jumpsuit uniform, Nameless headgear, The Surrealist charm and Fresh Paper weapon skin for the K1A.[1]

Trivia[]
The main menu is patterned after the openings of Season 1 of Money heist.
The event takes place on the map Bank.
The Hostage wears the jumpsuit with mask
One of the operators is a Korean which later references Money Heist Joint Economic Area.
Media[]
Images[]


Video[]
Tom Clancy’s Rainbow Six Siege – Netflix - La Casa de Papel (time-limited event)
External Links[]
Money Heist on the Rainbow Six Wiki

title: Watch Dogs: Legion
text:
Watch Dogs: Legion. Watch Dogs: Legion is an action adventure game that collaborated with Money Heist to make a free co-op mission based on the show. The free weekend was from Friday, September 3 - September 5, 2021.[1]

Contents

1 Description
2 Media

2.1 Images
2.2 Video


3 References


Description[]
The game can be played with up to three other players. The mission, titled Paperless, lets players don the series' signature masks and red jumpsuits to execute a crypto heist at the Bank of England. Clan Kelley force take over the bank and are storing millions of counterfeit ETO on the network. Players must sneak into the bank, disable the firewall, download the crypto, and make their escape across the Thames. Once completed, an exclusive skin for recruits will be unlocked.
The mission is available for Watch Dogs: Legion Online players.[2]

3 Trivia

Biography[]
Rafael, the prodigal son of Berlin, is an Electronics Engineers from MIT, Massachusetts with a post-degree in cybersecurity and has only one thing clear that he does not want to be a thief like his father.

Relationships[]
He is known to be a friend of The Professor and the son of Berlin.

Tatiana[]
During Tatiana and Berlin's marriage, Tatiana falls in love with Berlin's son, Rafael and divorces Berlin. As Berlin previously told Tatiana the plans of the heist and later denied her his inheritance, Tatiana and Rafael worked together to steal all the extracted gold (90 tons) from the robbers by having a group of people pretending to be police officers. The gold was buried in the ground and covered by a house and fake lawn. However, their plan does not work out as Alicia Sierra, who was working with The Professor, uncovers the hidden location and takes the gold back.

Trivia[]
Rafael is a fan of the composer Schumann.[1]

Benjamín Martínez, also known as Logroño, is a character in the Netflix series, Money Heist. He is the father of Manila and helps the Professor during the Bank of Spain Heist.[2]

Biography[]
Prior to the Bank of Spain heist, Benjamin is known to be a friend to Moscow 
Benjamín helps with the Bank of Spain Heist by working on the outside with Marseille, Boris and the Professor. Starting from making an escape point from a Chinese restaurant to  the Supreme court to save Lisbon.
His daughter Manila is a robber in the heist. According to Manila, Benjamín was initially not accepting of transgender people, which made it difficult for her to come out.

Physical Appearance[]
Benjamín is mostly bald with small amounts of grey hair. He is usually seen wearing glasses.

Personality[]
He is very keen on gender stereotypes such as, colors. When Marseille bought baby needs for baby Victoria, he got them all pink, in which Benjamin argued against it.

Relationships[]
Trivia[]
Logroño was the city name Benjamin was jokingly given by Alicia Sierra.
This is also reinforced as a name of the city in La Rioja, Spain.

title: Bella Ciao
text:
Bella Ciao. Bella Ciao is a song that is often featured in Money Heist. It is an Italian folk song that was sung by mondina workers to protest harsh working conditions. It was later adopted by resistant Italian partisans during the anti-fascist resistance in World War II.

Contents

1 In the series
2 Lyrics

2.1 Main Version
2.2 Sierra Version


3 References


In the series[]
In the show, the robbers sing this song as a symbol of resistance against the Spanish Police and Government. It is sung in several notable scenes. 
There arefive versions of this song: lively, long, funeral, percussive, credits. The lively version is heard when Moscow finds the second tunnel, the robbers all sing   Bella Ciao  together.[1]. The long version is heard in a flashback to the Toledo House, the Professor and Berlin perform "Bella Ciao" after a long discussion (long version)[2] It is also heard when Raquel and the Professor drive to the Toledo house. The funeral version is played on a harmonica by Helsinki as the doors of the bank are opened for the First missile. The credits version of Bella Ciao is sung on the End credits of Part 4 Plan Paris by Alicia Sierra. The final time we heard it is the percussive version which the robbers used cans as they sing at the Gold vault to raise their spirits.

Lyrics[]
Main Version[]
Una mattina mi son svegliato,
o bella ciao, bella ciao, bella ciao ciao ciao!
Una mattina mi son svegliato
e ho trovato l'invasor.
O partigiano portami via,
o bella ciao, bella ciao, bella ciao ciao ciao
o partigiano portami via
che mi sento di morir.
E se io muoio da partigiano,
o bella ciao, bella ciao, bella ciao ciao ciao,
e se io muoio da partigiano
tu mi devi seppellir.
Seppellire lassù in montagna,
o bella ciao, bella ciao, bella ciao ciao ciao,
seppellire lassù in montagna
sotto l'ombra di un bel fior.
E le genti che passeranno,
o bella ciao, bella ciao, bella ciao ciao ciao,
e le genti che passeranno
mi diranno «che bel fior.»
Questo è il fiore del partigiano,
o bella cia

title: Grândola, Vila Morena
text:
Grândola, Vila Morena. Grândola, Vila Morena is a song featured in Money heist.

Contents

1 About

1.1 Behind the song
1.2 In Money Heist
1.3 Lyrics




About[]
Behind the song[]
The song also represents Anti-Facists and Revolution to the Portuguese. it was being used as a radio-broadcast signal by the Portuguese Armed Forces Movement during their military coup operation in the morning of 25 April 1974, which lead to the Carnation Revolution and the transition to democracy in Portugal.

In Money Heist[]
The song is featured in Money Heist, where it is played as a requiem version. This is also the song played where Tokyo is making her last stand against the Spanish army.

Berlin is a spinoff about Berlin based on the Netflix series Money Heist. It was announced on December 1, 2021. The spinoff premiered on December 29, 2023.[1]
On February 20, 2024, Netflix confirmed that Berlin had been renewed for season 2.[2] Filming began in January 2025.[3]

Biography[]
Victoria was born in Part 5. As Alicia experienced complications during labour, Victoria was delivered by the Professor.
In Escape Valve, Alicia escaped with Victoria, who cried througout the car ride. When Alicia and the Professor hide in an apartment, they are almost caught by police when Victoria cries.

Physical Appearance[]
Victoria is a baby with brown eyes. She is often seen wearing a pink onesie.

Trivia[]
Victoria has a lot of meanings:
Roman Goddess of Victory
Capital City of Seychelles
Canadian Provincial capital
Australian state

Guna Yala is a location in Money Heist. It is an archipelago off Panama's eastern coast that contains more than 300 islands. It is where Rio and Tokyo hid after the Royal Mint of Spain Heist.

Contents

1 History
2 Inhabitants
3 Visitors
4 Trivia


History[]
Guna Yala is a place where Tokyo and Rio hide in secrecy after the Royal Mint of Spain Heist. When Tokyo left the island to party in the city, Rio attempts to call her with a phone that he believed to be unregistered. However, it was registered and lead Interpol right to his location. They arrest Rio and this kicks off the Bank of Spain Heist.

Inhabitants[]
Rio (formerly)
Tokyo (formerly)
Visitors[]
Interpol officers
Trivia[]
The island is shot at the actual location as seen in Money Heist: The Phenomenon.

History[]
A year after the Royal Mint of Spain Heist, Raquel gets a coordinates in a series of letters with the coordinates of Palawan. She later met the Profesor at a bar in Palawan, the same way she met him in Madrid. Later Raquel's family move there  alongside the Professor. 
During the Bank of Spain heist, Raquel's family hde in a different part of the Philippines.

Agustin Ramos, known by his code name Moscow (  Spanish:   Moscú ), was one of the main protagonists in Part 1 and 2 of Money Heist. He was one of the eight robbers who went in to rob the Royal Mint of Spain and the father of Denver, another protagonist in the show.

Contents

1 Biography
2 Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships

4.1 Denver
4.2 Tokyo


5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
His first job was a miner in Asturias. The job caused him to get silicosis, but what caused him to quit was claustrophobia, as he couldn't stand being in a small hole.[3] He later became a thief and was sent to prison several times.
Little is known about Moscow's childhood, but we find out in Part 2 that when his son, Denver, was very young, his wife was struggling with a drug addiction. Moscow revealed that he left his wife at a roundabout to pick up her drugs, but left with Denver before she returned, and moved elsewhere.
He explains that he regrets that decision and went back many times to try and find her, but never

Berlin (  Korean:   베를린 ) is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist: Korea - Joint Economic Area, portrayed by actor Park Hae-soo.

Contents

1 Biography
2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships
5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
Song Jung-Ho was born in Taechon, Pyeongan Province.[1]
When he was 9, Berlin and his mother attempted to flee North Korea by swimming across the Amrok river between China and North Korea at night. During their escape, they were spotted by soldiers, who shot and killed his mother. 
Berlin was taken to a prison camp, where he was subjected to forced labour and physical abuse. He was beaten by other prisoners and in a fit of rage, murdered another prisoner. He was put into solitary confinement. Over the years, he gained a following. Under his orders, prisoners rioted and took over the prison. A guard was murdered, setting Berlin free, after 25 years in the prison camp.[2]

Physical Appearance[]
Berlin is a tall, slim middle aged man with who, when in the heist process itself is wearing a red coveralls with a military grad Load Bearing Equipment, also known as the LBE

Personality[]
Relationships[]
Trivia[]
This is the Second time Park Hae-Soo's character is involved in a Netflix production involves a masked armed person with a jumpsuit. The first was in Squid Game.
The irony is he is one of the main characters who is a hero.

title: Korea Unified Mint Heist

Korea Unified Mint Heist






Type
Heist


Location
Korea Unified Mint

The Professor


Korea Unified Mint Heist is a heist that took place at the Korea Unified Mint.

Contents

1 Context
2 Notable Participants

2.1 Robbers
2.2 Hostages
2.3 Police


3 References


Context[]
North and South Korea are on the verge of a peaceful reunification after nearly 80 years of division. As the nations prepare to print a brand-new unified currency, a heist is hatched. A genius strategist known as the Professor assembles a motley crew of top-tier thieves from north and south in an attempt to steal the new currency straight from the mint.
The Professor who approaches Tokyo with a plan to steal 4 trillion won from the Korea Unified Mint. He assembles a top-class group of thieves to pull it off, but chaos ensues as hostages are taken and a joint task force led by South Korean negotiation specialist Seon Woojin and former North Korean special agent Cha Moohyuk is formed to stop them.

Biography[]
Cho Youngmin is the director of the Korea Unified Mint who had an affair with Yun Misun. When Misun became pregnant, Youngmin denied being the father, as he claimed to have had a vasectomy 10 years prior.
During the Korean police's attempted infiltration, Youngmin and another hostage, the daughter of a US ambassador, appeared in a YouTube livestream. They begged the police not to use force as the hostages were dressed in the same jumpsuits as the robbers and were all carrying guns, making it impossible for the police to differentiate the robbers and hostages.[1]

Physical Appearance[]
Cho Youngmin has short dark hair.

Personality[]
Cho Youngmin is possessive and controlling. He was seen physically forcing himself onto Yun Misun as soon as she entered his office. He is also seen giving orders to other hostages and expecting them to be followed without question.

Biography[]
Cha Moohyuk is the captain of the Ministry of People Security of the DPRK.

Physical Appearance[]
Personality[]
Relationships[]
Trivia[]
Cha Moohyuk is not based on any specific character from the original series. However, a Netflix article introducing the main characters describes Moohyuk as being similar to Alfonso Prieto, as he is oppositional toward Inspector Seon Woojin, he dismisses negotiating with the robbers and opts for swift and unrestrained use of force to deal with the hostage situation.[1]

Tokyo (  Korean:   도쿄 ) is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist: Korea - Joint Economic Area, portrayed by actor Jun Jong-seo.

Contents

1 Biography
2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships
5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
Tokyo grew up in Pyeongyang, North Korea. She was a fan of BTS and would secretly listen to their music in her room. She later joined the North Korean army and fought in the war against South Korea.
After the reunification of North and South Korea, Tokyo moved from Pyeongyang to Seoul. An immigration broker deceived her, leading Tokyo to lose both her job and her home. She began waitressing at a restaurant and performing at Club Moranbong. Her boss encourage her to try the drug ice.
Suddenly, several people burst into the room. A woman covered in blood collapsed on the floor. The men explained that she tried to escape without paying off her debts. Her arm was covered in needle wounds. Tokyo suddenly realized that the girl was her former co-worker at a restaurant.
Tokyo accused the men of being thieves and was slapped. Her boss straddled her, but Tokyo managed to push him off. During the altercation, he pushed her head through a window, and she shot him with a gun. After shooting the other men, she invited the other girl to escape with her.[1]
When the girl dies after getting shot, she manages to escape, and is found by The Professor.

Season 1 of Netflix's Money Heist: Korea - Joint Economic Area consisted of 12 episodes, and  aired on June 24, 2022.

Contents

1 Synopsis
2 Plot
3 Cast

3.1 Main Cast


4 Episodes
5 Media

6 References


Synopsis[]
Thieves overtake the mint of a unified Korea. With hostages trapped inside, the police must stop them — as well as the shadowy mastermind behind it all.

Plot[]
North and South Korea are on the verge of a peaceful reunification after nearly 80 years of division. As the nations prepare to print a brand-new unified currency, a heist is hatched. A genius strategist known as the Professor assembles a motley crew of top-tier thieves from north and south in an attempt to steal the new currency straight from the mint.
The Professor (Yoo Ji-tae) who approaches Tokyo (Jun Jong-seo) with a plan to steal 4 trillion won from the Korea Unified Mint. He assembles a top-class group of thieves to pull it off, but chaos ensues as hostages are taken and a joint task force led by South Korean negotiation specialist Seon Woojin (Kim Yunjin) and former North Korean special agent Cha Moohyuk (Kim Seung-o) is formed to stop them.[1]

Synopsis[]
Recruited by the Professor for a job of unprecedented proportions, eight thieves storm the Unified Korea Mint. Police pull together a task force.

Plot[]
Tokyo lives in Pyeongyang, North Korea, with her mother. She is a fan of BTS and secretly listens to their music in her room. She later joins the North Korean army and fights in the war against South Korea.
After the reunification of North and South Korea, Tokyo moves from Pyeongyang to Seoul. An immigration broker deceives her, leading Tokyo to lose both her job and her home. She begins waitressing at a restaurant and performing at Club Moranbong. Her boss encourages her to try the drug ice.
Suddenly, several people burst into the room. A woman covered in blood collapses on the floor. The men explain that she tried to escape without paying off her debts. Her arm was covered in needle wounds. Tokyo suddenly realizes that the girl was her former co-worker at a restaurant.
Tokyo accuses the men of being thieves and is slapped. Her boss straddles her, but Tokyo manages to push him off. During the altercation, he pushes her head through a window, and she shoots him with a gun. After shooting the other men, she invited the other girl to escape with her.
One year later, Tokyo is out in the rain at night. She sees a news report on TV saying that she is a wanted by police for a series of robberies against loan sharks. The reporter on TV claims that crimes related to illegal immigrants have been increasing due to the rapid North-South economic expansion, leading to an influx of low-wage workers and widening the wealth gap.

title: Hahoetal Mask
text:
Hahoetal Mask. Haoetal Mask






Type
Mask (Clothing)


Source
Based On Hahoe byeolsingut talnori


The Hahoetal Mask mask is the signature look of the Robbers in Korea Unified Mint Heist.
The masks are based on  the traditional Korean masks worn in the Hahoe Pyolshin-gut t'al nori ceremony dating back to the 12th century.

See Also[]
Dali Mask - Spanish counterpart used in the original series.

Martín Berrote, also known by his alias, Palermo, is one of the main characters in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actor Rodrigo de la Serna.

1 Biography

1.1 Part 3


2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships

4.1 Berlin
4.2 Helsinki


5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
He is born in Buenos Aires, in Argentina and has known Berlin for at least 10 years. Prior to the second heist, he had lived in Palermo, Italy. He is a trained engineer.

Part 3[]
Although he was not part of the Royal Mint of Spain heist, he was an acquaintance of Berlin and The Professor prior to that. He collaborated with Berlin for coming up with the Bank of Spain heist and they both presented the plan to The Professor, who was uncertain about it. The Professor had his doubts about Palermo, claiming he cared more about his love for Berlin — with whom he exchanged a kiss — than the plan itself.
After the robbery of the Royal Mint, when the crew needed a way to rescue Rio from captivity, The Professor started organizing the Bank of Spain heist, bringing Pal

Contents

1 Biography
2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality

Biography[]
Cameron is the most recent recruit to Berlin's crew. She is the group's femme fatale and described by Berlin as "pure adrenaline" and "a ticking time bomb that's impossible to defuse." Cameron's reasons for being a part of this heist are her drive for excitement and something more psychologically complex. Cameron was previously in a mental institution.
There is a spark between Cameron and Roi, a career criminal with a talent for lockpicking.
Both Cameron and Berlin are running from their past.

Physical Appearance[]
Cameron has long, dark and wavy hair. She has a tattoo on her left thigh that says, "DON'T look at me."

Personality[]
Relationships[]
Trivia[]
Cameron's last-minute addition to Berlin's crew mirrors the last-minute addition of Tokyo to The Professor's team in the original series.

Roi is one of the main characters in the Netflix spinoff series, Berlin, portrayed by Julio Peña.

Contents

1 Biography
2 Physical Appearance
3 Personality
4 Relationships
5 Trivia
6 Appearances
7 Gallery
8 References


Biography[]
Roi is a career criminal with a talent for lock-picking and for getting arrested. He has worked with Berlin since his teenage years. 
Roi is fiercely loyal to Berlin, his mentor, and always strives to obey every rule of a good heist. In Berlin's words, Roi looks up to him as "a father he never had" while Berlin looks at him "like his loyal dog he takes for walks every day."
However, a spark with Cameron, a newcomer and femme fatale in Berlin's crew, proves challenging to resist.

Physical Appearance[]
Roi has a tattoo of an octopus on his upper back.[3]
Roi always wears a black glove on his left hand. He has a tattoo on his right hand.

Biography[]
Damián is a close confidant of Berlin. A professor by day, he moonlights as a criminal mastermind. Alongside Berlin, Damián is the brains of the operation. He is also a mentor figure to members of Berlin's crew and takes the rules more seriously than Berlin.
Berlin describes Damián as, "like most geniuses, he is a scatterbrain."

The Vienot Auction House, also known as Chez Vienot, is an auction house. The director is François Polignac.

History[]
The Vienot Auction House was robbed by Berlin's crew, after a shipment of valuable jewellery was stored in the vault. As the crew robbed the vault without a trace and manipulated the security camera footage, François was arrested as the primary suspect.

Season 2 of Netflix's Berlin consists of 8 episodes.
On February 20, 2024, Netflix confirmed that Berlin had been renewed for season 2.[1] Filming began in January 2025.[2]
In season 2, the characters carry out a heist in Seville. There, Berlin and his gang will encounter new characters such as Candela, an unpredictable and temperamental Sevillian woman played by Inma Cuesta, the eccentric and hedonistic Duke of Malaga Álvaro Hermoso de Medina, played by José Luis García-Pérez, and his mysterious and refined Duchess of Malaga Genoveva Dante, played by Marta Nieto.[2]

Alison Parker is a secondary character in the Netflix series Money Heist, portrayed by actress María Pedraza. She is one of the hostages that were held in the Royal Mint of Spain.

Biography[]
Alison is the daughter of Sir Benjamin Parker, the British Ambassador in Spain. Her father has taken her hunting regularly since she was five years old, but what she really wants to do is dance.[3]

Part 1[]
Alison was a student visiting the Royal Mint of Spain with her classmates from Brighton College when the heist took place. Unbeknownst to her, she was instrumental to the timing of the heist, as The Professor knew she would be an important hostage.
Because she was so important, Arturo told her to hide while Inspector Raquel was in the Royal Mint of Spain to check if everybody is alive. If she wouldn't be found, police would enter the Mint. She was found by Nairobi.

Physical Appearance[]
Alison has curly brown hair.

Personality[]
Alison is shy and has low self-esteem. She becomes a target for her classmates who constantly harass her.

Pedro González Alonso (born June 21, 1971) is a Spanish actor who portrays Berlin in the series Money Heist. 
He is best known for his roles in Rías Baixas (2003–2005), Maridos e mulleres (2006–2008), Father Casares (2014–2015) and Gondar (2009), at a local level in Galicia. He is also known for playing characters such as Diego Murquía/Adrián Vera Celande in Gran Hotel.
In 2018, he was chosen as "International Star of the Year" by GQ Turkey magazine. Alonso also co-starred in Diablo Guardian, Amazon Prime Video’s first drama series in Latin America, which was released in May 2018.

Úrsula Corberó Delgado (born August 11, 1989) is a Spanish actress who portrays Tokyo in the series Money Heist.
She is best known for her roles as Ruth Gomez in the Antena 3 television series Física o Química (2008-2010), Margarita de Austria in the Televisión Española television series Isabel (2014) and Marta in the comedy film Cómo Sobrevivir a una Despedida (2015).
She is the face of several brands, including Bulgari since 2018.

Early life[]
Úrsula Corberó Delgado was born in Barcelona. She grew up in Sant Pere de Vilamajor with her parents, Pedro Corberó, a carpenter and Esther Delgado, a shopkeeper. She has one sister named Mónica.
By age 6, she already knew she wanted to be an actress and she first started acting in commercials. She got her first part at age 13 and took acting classes as well as flamenco and jazz dance classes. After her studies, she moved to Madrid to shoot the TV series Física o Química.
She speaks Catalan and Spanish fluently.

About Money Heist

Money Heist (also known as La Casa de Papel) is a Spanish television crime-drama series.
An enigmatic man, nicknamed The Professor, plans the biggest heist in history. To carry out this plan, he recruits eight of the country's top criminals who have nothing to lose: Tokyo, an experienced robber, Berlin, the ringleader, Moscow, the drilling expert, Rio, the computer scientist, Nairobi, the counterfeiter, Denver, son of Moscow, and Helsinki and Oslo, the Balkan war veterans.
The goal is to infiltrate the Royal Mint of Spain in order to print €2.4 billion in less than 11 days, without spelling a single drop of blood. The group takes 67 hostages, including the daughter of the British Ambassador. With the mastermind working on the outside, they always manage to stay one step ahead of the police.

Part 1 (also referred to as the first part of season 1) of Netflix's Money Heist consisted of 13 episodes, and  aired on December 20, 2017.

Contents

1 Synopsis
2 Plot
3 Cast

3.1 Main Cast
3.2 Recurring


4 Episodes
5 Media

5.1 Video




Synopsis[]
Eight thieves take hostages and lock themselves in the Royal Mint of Spain as a criminal mastermind manipulates the police to carry out his plan.

Plot[]
Part 1 begins with the aftermath of a failed bank robbery by a woman named "Tokyo", as a man named the "Professor" saves her from being caught by the police and proposes to her a heist of drastic proportions. After a brief outline of the planned heist, the story jumps to the beginning of a multi-day assault on the Royal Mint of Spain in Madrid. The eight robbers are code-named after cities: Tokyo, Moscow, Berlin, Nairobi, Rio, Denver, Helsinki and Oslo. 
Dressed in red jumpsuits with a mask of the Spanish painter Salvador Dalí, the group of robbers take 67 hostages as part of their plan to print and escape with €2.4 billion through a self-built escape tunnel. The Professor heads the heist from an external location. Flashbacks throughout the series show the five months of preparation in an abandoned hunting estate in the Toledo countryside; the robbers are not to share personal information nor engage in personal relationships, and the assault shall be without bloodshed.

Synopsis[]
The Professor recruits a young female robber and seven other criminals for a grand heist, targeting the Royal Mint of Spain.

Plot[]



!

Warning: This section contains spoilers.

A young criminal is on the run, wanted by all the police in Spain. She makes a last call to her mother before leaving the country. A stranger approaches her in the street and proposes that she join his heist - where he plans to print €2.4 billion. She accepts, and finds herself with seven other robbers in a house in Toledo, where a man named the Professor prepares them for the heist for five months. To remain anonymous, each robber chooses a city as a code-name, and she becomes Tokyo. She will carry out this heist alongside Berlin, a jewelry thief, Moscow, a specialist in mechanics, and his son Denver, who has a penchant for fighting. She also meets Rio, a young hacker, Oslo and Helsinki, two Serbs, and Nairobi, a counterfeiter. The Professor announces to them that they are going to rob the Royal Mint of Spain.
On the day of the robbery, a group of high school students from Brighton College visit the mint. One of the students, Alison Parker, is the daughter of the British Ambassador, who is a close friend of the Queen of England. At the same time, Mónica Gaztambide, an employee of the mint, announces to her lover, Arturo Román, who is none other than the director, that she is pregnant with his child. It is at this moment, the eight robbers hijack the truck that is r

Synopsis[]
Hostage negotiator Raquel makes initial contact with the Professor. One of the hostages is a crucial part of the thieves' plans.

Plot[]



!

Warning: This section contains spoilers.

Raquel Murillo, divorced police inspector, celebrates her daughter's birthday. Shortly after, she is sent by her boss deal with the heist at the Royal Mint of Spain.
In the Royal Mint of Spain, tensions are high as Tokyo shot at police, allowing her affair with Rio to endanger The Professor's plans. Shortly after a discussion about women with Berlin, Rio is tasked with separating Alison Parker from the group. All hostages are given a red jumpsuit and a fake gun. The employees of the Royal Mint of Spain are also sent to print as many €50 notes as possible.
Raquel Murillo and her colleague Ángel Rubio monitor the telephone network of the mint. However, Colonel Prieto of the Spanish intelligence has no intention of letting Raquel run everything. The police start a negotiation with the Professor, who advises police not to intervene. Raquel waits for the robbers to free the high school students. This is when we learn that one of the hostages, Alison Parker, is the daughter of the Ambassador of the United Kingdom, so it a priority to rescue her to prevent diplomatic issues. Colonel Prieto decides to enter the mint, as the Professor had predicted. However, after inserting an endoscope camera, the police realise that the robbers are m

Synopsis[]
Police grab an image of the face of one of the robbers. Raquel is suspicious of the gentleman she meets at a bar.

Plot[]



!

Warning: This section contains spoilers.

Inspector Murillo, tired of the conflicts with Colonel Prieto, calls her boss at six in the morning from the Bar Hanoi to resign. Unfortunately for her, her phone has no more battery, but a stranger, who is none other than the Professor, offers her his phone. Later, her boss calls her back and convinces her to return to work.
In the Royal Mint of Spain, the robbers have already managed to print €52 million and continue with their plan. A hostage, Mónica Gaztambide, is responsible for reading the police a press release from the robbers, which specifies that the hostages are being treated well, and above all, that they cannot enter the mint without risking injuring hostages. Ángel Rubio orders police not to shoot, contrary to what Colonel Prieto had hoped.
Berlin recruits a few hostages (including Arturo Román and Pablo Ruiz) to dig a tunnel in the basement of the building. Soon after, Alison Parker  who wants to delete a private photo of herself on the Internet, manages to get her phone while Tokyo and Rio are busy arguing. This allows the police to hack into the camera of Alison's smartphone, and capture a photo of Rio's face. Berlin decides to punish Rio for his carelessness and orders Oslo and Helsinki to beat h

Synopsis[]
Raquel suffers a personal crisis with her ex. The hostages attempt to contact polices, but they are frightened by the gunshots they overheard. The thieves argue among themselves.

Plot[]



!

Warning: This section contains spoilers.

Denver is ordered to kill Mónica Gaztambide for concealing a cell phone. However, he can't bring himself to do it. Denver instead shoots Mónica in the thigh to make it appear that he killed her, and he hides her in a secluded vault.
The police calls the Professor to convince him to surrender, however the Professor refuses to. A few moments later, Raquel receives a call from her mother, who has Alzheimer's. Alberto Vicuña, her ex-husband, has taken their daughter Paula out, despite the restraining order against him. Alberto returns some time later with Paula.
To breathe a bit, Raquel talks to Salva at the Bar Hanoi. She tells him that her ex-husband beat her for several years, and that she did not dare to file a complaint because he was also a police officer and was admired by everyone the police station.
Following Mónica's fake execution, the robbers, who heard the gunshots, attack Berlin. He explains that he had no choice and the others are completely lost, since the rule of "no blood" has been broken. Synopsis[]
The thieves let a medical team enter the Mint, and an undercover policeman sneaks in with them. Can the Professor stay one step ahead of Raquel?

Plot[]



!

Warning: This section contains spoilers.

While Arturo Román, who is injured by the police, bleeds out, the robbers all try to operate on him. During the few months in Toledo, the Professor taught them some basic first aid and anatomical knowledge. However, practicing on a body is easier than removing a bullet from a live person, so the robbers decide to wait. On the other hand, Moscow and Denver try to operate Mónica, whose thigh still contains the bullet that Denver had shot into her. 
Raquel Murillo decides to take full responsibility for this accident. She then begins a new telephone discussion with the Professor, and the two agree to send a medical team into the Royal Mint of Spain. But Raquel is completely unsettled by the accident she has just caused, and her mother comforts her over the phone. Her mother also learns that she is seeing a man.
Ángel Rubio decides to enter the mint by pretending to be a nurse that is with the medical team coming to treat Arturo. At the same time, Suárez and an agent will infiltrate through the underground conduits, dressed in red suits and masks to assimilate with the hostages.
Arturo asks to call his wife but due to nervousness, he accidentally calls her "Mónica". When the doctors enter the Factory, the P
